# One-off — CHRISTUS SPOHN HOSPITAL CORPUS CHRISTI (TX)

```
**To:** Chief Executive Officer, CHRISTUS SPOHN HOSPITAL CORPUS CHRISTI
**CC:** Chief Operating Officer; Director of Safety & Security

**Subject:** SB 240 compliance + recent OSHA incident — Ember + Lattice assessment

Your facility reported a severe intracranial injury in the past 24 months. Under Texas Health & Safety Code Ch. 331 (effective Sept 2024), you're required to demonstrate workplace violence prevention and rapid response capability across your 1040-bed acute care and behavioral unit operations.

Firefly's Ember software + Lattice panic buttons enable staff-initiated detection, precise location data, and coordinated alert workflows—meeting SB 240's documentation and response-time requirements while addressing the incident patterns OSHA flagged.

Would you have 15 minutes to review how peer safety-net hospitals in your cohort are approaching this?

— Ryan

---
**Why this opening:** SB 240's Sept 2024 effective date is now in-force compliance territory, and the recent OSHA-documented severe injury (intracranial hemorrhage) signals an acute vulnerability the mandate directly targets—creating urgency without manufactured alarm.
```
